source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with added capabilities
input_row.description: Do	not	generally	permit	containers	with	capabilities	assigned	beyond	the	default	set.
input_row.rationale: Containers	run	with	a	default	set	of	capabilities	as	assigned	by	the	Container	Runtime. Capabilities	outside	this	set	can	be	added	to	containers	which	could	expose	them	to	risks	of container	breakout	attacks. There	should	be	at	least	one	PodSecurityPolicy	(PSP)	defined	which	prevents	containers with	capabilities	beyond	the	default	set	from	launching. If	you	need	to	run	containers	with	additional	capabilities,	this	should	be	defined	in	a separate	PSP	and	you	should	carefully	check	RBAC	controls	to	ensure	that	only	limited service	accounts	and	users	are	given	permission	to	access	that	PSP. Impact: Pods	with	containers	which	require	capabilities	outwith	the	default	set	will	not	be permitted.
input_row.audit: Get	the	set	of	PSPs	with	the	following	command: kubectl get psp Verify	that	there	are	no	PSPs	present	which	have	allowedCapabilities	set	to	anything other	than	an	empty	array.
input_row.remediation: Ensure	that	allowedCapabilities	is	not	present	in	PSPs	for	the	cluster	unless	it	is	set	to	an empty	array. Default	Value: 228	|	P a g e By	default,	PodSecurityPolicies	are	not	defined. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy/#enabling-pod- security-policies 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and- unprivileged-linux-containers/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 229	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: Pods	with	containers	which	require	capabilities	outwith	the	default	set	will	not	be permitted.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy/#enabling-pod- security-policies 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and- unprivileged-linux-containers/ CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 229	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control focuses on examining PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs) for containers with added capabilities, beyond the default set. This requires listing and analyzing current PSP configurations to verify that `allowedCapabilities` is set correctly across them. At present, there are no direct Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI commands that can automate the verification of specific PSP configurations within ACK clusters to the required level of detail regarding specific capabilities.

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
- The control requires specific checks of Kubernetes PSP configuration fields (`allowedCapabilities`), which are policy-level and require interpretation.
- The given audit instructions specify using `kubectl` to retrieve PSPs, rather than any Aliyun CLI/Alibaba Cloud CLI commands. This suggests a more manual review process in interpreting the retrieved data to ensure compliance.
- The complexity and nature of Kubernetes PSPs generally fall under a policy management task, often better suited for manual inspection due to the contextual and organizational understanding needed.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:**
   - Use the Kubernetes command-line tool `kubectl` to authenticate and connect to your ACK cluster.

2. **Retrieve Pod Security Policies (PSPs):**
   - Execute the command: `kubectl get psp` to list all current PodSecurityPolicies on your cluster.

3. **Verify allowedCapabilities Settings:**
   - Review each PSP to ensure that the `allowedCapabilities` field is either not present or set to an empty array.
   - Special attention should be given to any PSP allowing additional capabilities. Document any discrepancies with expected configurations.

4. **Confirm Security Posture:**
   - Verify the cluster has PSP with strict policies where `allowedCapabilities` is minimal or empty, consistent with best practices.
   - Confirm that any use of added capabilities is strictly controlled via Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and only granted to trusted service accounts/users, as per organizational security policies.

5. **Document Findings:**
   - Record the configurations and results of the manual verification, including any deviations from expected posture and proposed corrective actions if necessary.
